title: sagina kevandensis
intro: Sagina kevandensis, also known as the Iranian pearlwort, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family. This perennial plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in traditional Iranian and Middle Eastern healing practices. The medicinal properties of Sagina kevandensis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's health benefits are said to include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, and it is often prepared as an herbal tea or tincture for these purposes.
